paper_title: Thermal and Rheological Analysis of PLA/PBAT Blends with Talc and Diatomite

methods.description: Differential Scanning Calorimetry (DSC) was used to study the thermal properties of PLA/PBAT blends with or without particles, focusing on glass transition and crystallization temperatures.

steps.description: DSC curves are shown in Figure 2, and data calculated from these curves are presented in Table 1.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain DSC curves and calculated data

steps.description: Analyze the glass transition temperature (Tg) of PLA and PBAT from the second heating and cooling ramp.
outputs: Tg values for PLA and PBAT

steps.description: Evaluate the effect of talc or diatomite on the Tg of PLA in the blend.
Determine the crystallization temperature (Tc) and crystallinity of PLA and PBAT.

methods.description: Thermogravimetric Analysis (TGA) was used to evaluate the influence of talc and diatomite on the thermal stability of PLA/PBAT blends.

steps.description: TGA thermograms of the blends with talc and diatomite incorporation ranging from 1% to 7% are shown in Figure 3a.
Analyze the initial decomposition temperature of the PLA/PBAT blends.
Evaluate the effect of talc and diatomite on the thermal stability of the blends.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate particle effect on thermal stability
outputs: effect of particles on thermal stability

methods.description: Dynamic rheological tests were conducted to study the viscosity, phase morphology, and interfacial actions in PLA/PBAT blends with talc and diatomite.

steps.description: Evaluate the elastic modulus G′, loss modulus G″, and complex viscosity η* versus frequency.
steps.searchable_summary: measure G′, G″, and η* versus frequency

steps.description: Analyze the effect of particles on G′, G″, and η*.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze particle effect on rheological properties
outputs: effect of particles on rheological properties

steps.description: Use cole-cole plot to characterize the different structures of the blends.
